A strong desire to serve God led the Israelites, on some occasions, to dedicate themselves or their children to serving the Lord, in his house for life. Some people who dedicated themselves this way might be employed as assistants; generally they were to be redeemed for a price. It is good to be enthusiastically and generously disposed toward the Lord's service; but the matter should be carefully considered, and prudence should guide what we do; otherwise hasty promises and hesitation in fulfilling them will dishonor God and trouble our own minds.

Our houses, lands, cattle, and all our possessions must be used to the glory of God. It is pleasing to him that a portion be given to support his worship and to promote his cause. But God would not approve such zeal as ruined a man's family.

Things or people devoted are distinguished from things or people that were only set apart. Devoted things were most holy to the Lord, and could neither be taken back nor used for other purposes. Whatever they had, God must be honored with one-tenth of it, if it could be applied. In this way they acknowledge God to be the Owner of their land, the Giver of its fruits, and themselves to be his tenants and dependent on him. In this way they gave him thanks for the abundance they enjoyed and asked for his favor in continuing it. We are taught to honor the Lord with our possessions.

The last verse seems to refer to this whole book. Many of the instructions in it are moral, and always binding; others are ceremonial, and specific to the Jewish nation; yet they have a spiritual meaning, and so teach us; for to us also, by these institutions, the gospel is preached. The doctrine of reconciliation to God by a Mediator is not obscured by the smoke of burning sacrifices, but made clear by knowledge of Christ and him crucified. We are under the gentle and easy instructions of the gospel, which declares those true worshippers who worship the Father in spirit and truth, through Christ only, and in his name. Yet, let us not think that because we are not bound to ceremonial rites and offerings, a little care, time, and expense will be enough to honor God with. Having boldness to enter into the holiest by the blood of Jesus, let us draw near with a true heart, and in full assurance of faith, worshipping God with greater joyfulness and humble confidence, still saying, BLESSED BE GOD FOR JESUS CHRIST.
